title: Stool-Ball.
content:
At stool-ball, Lucia, let us play
    For sugar-cakes and wine:
    Or for a tansy let us pay,
    The loss, or thine, or mine.

    If thou, my dear, a winner be
    At trundling of the ball,
    The wager thou shall have, and me,
    And my misfortunes all.

    But if, my sweetest, I shall get,
    Then I desire but this:
    That likewise I may pay the bet
    And have for all a kiss.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Stool-Ball."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
